text: BREAKING: IRGC gunboats fire on THREE container ships in Strait of Hormuz — Epaminondas sustains 'heavy damage to bridge' from gunfire and RPGs ~15nm NE of Oman. MSC Francesca fired upon ~6nm off Iran. Eurphoria attacked 8nm west of Iran. IRGC then SEIZES MSC Francesca and Epaminondas, tows to Iranian waters. No crew casualties. Biggest naval escalation since ceasefire began.
verificationNote: Confirmed by Seatrade Maritime, CNBC, UKMTO, ABC News, BNO News.

text: HORMUZ: IRGC Navy claimed vessels were 'attempting to secretly exit' and 'manipulating navigation systems.' Ships towed to Iranian waters to 'examine cargo and documents.' War-risk premiums at 2.5% hull value (60x pre-crisis). Only 3 vessels crossed Monday. Bessent warns Kharg Island storage 'full in days' — Iranian oil wells face shutdown.
verificationNote: IRGC claims from Iran International/Tasnim. Bessent from FXStreet. Insurance data from WEF report.

text: LEBANON: Hezbollah fires rockets/drone at IDF in Rab al-Thalathine for 2nd consecutive day. Drone intercepted near Kfar Yuval. IDF struck launcher within minutes. IDF Chief Zamir places military on 'high alert' — ready to return to fighting on all fronts. Death toll raised to 2,454 killed, 7,658 wounded. Israel-Lebanon talks set Thursday in DC.
verificationNote: Times of Israel, JPost, Asharq Al-Awsat confirmed. Zamir from Ynet News.

text: MARKETS: Brent $99.81 (+1.4%, +25.0% from pre-war). WTI $90.86 (+20.3%). Oil rising on IRGC ship attacks. Only 3 vessels crossed Hormuz on Monday. Iran has ~190M barrels floating storage (~$15B deferred revenue). IEA confirms 10.1M bpd disruption — largest in history. Gold $4,769.93. Shekel below 3.0/dollar (30-year high).
verificationNote: CNBC confirmed oil prices. IEA Oil Market Report April 2026. Times of Israel/Haaretz confirmed shekel.

text: DIPLOMACY: Iran fully boycotts talks — Vance Pakistan trip postponed indefinitely. Araghchi: blockade is 'act of war.' Iran advisor Mohammadi: ceasefire extension is 'meaningless,' 'time for Iran to take initiative.' IRGC commander Mousavi threatens Gulf states: 'bid farewell to oil production' if facilities used against Iran. China warns 'critical juncture.'
verificationNote: Axios, Al Jazeera, The Hill confirmed. IRGC threat from Naharnet/Times of Israel. China from Al Jazeera liveblog.

text: Day 54 AM roundup: IRGC fires on 3 ships, seizes 2 in Hormuz (Epaminondas, MSC Francesca). Trump: Iran 'collapsing financially,' losing $500M/day. Iran boycotts talks. Pentagon FY2027: $1.5T (drones tripled, Tomahawks 14x). EU agrees Hormuz sanctions. Lebanese death toll 2,454. Hezbollah rockets Day 2. Iranian drones hit Kurdish PAK bases. Brent $99.81. Gas $4.022.
verificationNote: Compiled from Seatrade Maritime, CNBC, ANI News, Axios, Military.com, Asharq Al-Awsat, Gulan Media, AAA. All verified.

text: ECONOMICS: Pentagon FY2027 budget released — $1.5T total. Drone spending TRIPLED to $74B. Tomahawk orders 14x (55→785). $55B for Defense Autonomous Warfare Group. Harvard estimate: war will cost $1T over next decade. Daily burn rate ~$2B/day. New OFAC sanctions: 7 individuals, 4 entities, 2 Mahan Air aircraft. Iran sanctions waiver expired April 19.
verificationNote: Military.com, Scripps News, US News confirmed budget. CNBC/Fortune confirmed Harvard estimate. OFAC from GovPing.

text: WEST BANK: Settlers set Palestinian home on fire in Beit Imrin near Nablus — 8 injured including 1-year-old baby. Family surrounded, blocked from escaping. Turkey condemned. Also: Israeli army reservist killed 2 Palestinians (14-year-old, 32-year-old) in al-Mughayyir April 21. 16-year-old killed by vehicle escorting Israeli minister in Hebron.
verificationNote: Washington Post, CNN, CBC News confirmed. Turkey MFA statement No. 73.

text: HORMUZ UPDATE: Both seized vessels (MSC Francesca, Epaminondas) remain stationary west of Iranian port Sirik per AIS. Epaminondas sustained heavy bridge damage from IRGC gunfire at 03:55 UTC 15nm NE of Oman; later resumed AIS transmission. Greek Shipping Ministry disputes full seizure — claims ship damaged but not boarded. US-chartered VLCC Hormuz insurance: $10–14M per transit. War-risk premium 1.5–3% of hull value.
verificationNote: Lloyd's List (LL1156987, LL1156586, LL1156502), NPR. AIS tracking independent confirmation.

text: OIL: Brent surges past $101.14 (+26.7%) on IRGC ship seizures; intraday peak $101.86. WTI $91.81 (+21.6%). Demand destruction estimate 4–5M bpd (~5% of global supply) — Asia absorbing most. Kpler: Iran exported 1.71M bpd in April pre-blockade; earned $4.97B March 15–April 14. Iran losing ~$150M/day from oil exports under US blockade. ~190M barrels floating at sea, mostly China-bound.
verificationNote: Fortune, CNBC, BNN Bloomberg, Kpler, Al Jazeera, The National. All verified.

text: IRAN INTERNET: Nationwide blackout has now entered Day 54 — connectivity cut since war began Feb 28. Prevents independent verification of casualties and strike damage. HRANA and Al-Monitor warn 'true casualty figures unknown.' Iranian forensics chief Masjedi April 20: 3,375 killed (2,875 men, 496 women, 383 children under 18, 4 unidentified) since Feb 28.
verificationNote: NetBlocks monitoring (via Al Jazeera liveblog), Washington Times/AP, Al-Monitor. HRANA tracker.

text: JORDAN: Chinese HD satellite imagery (April 19) reveals US has used the ceasefire to reposition THAAD launchers and AN/TPY-2 radars, plus Patriot PAC-3 batteries and AN/MPQ-65 radars across Jordan. Recall: a THAAD radar (~$300M) was destroyed at Muwaffaq Salti Air Base earlier in the war. Jerusalem Post reports interceptor stockpiles 'critically low.'
verificationNote: Defence Security Asia (Chinese satellite imagery analysis), Jerusalem Post stockpile reporting.

text: DAY 54 MIDDAY: Brent $101.14 (+26.7%) — intraday peak $101.86. WTI $91.81. EU formally widens sanctions — Kallas announces IRGC terrorist designation. Araghchi: US blockade 'act of war' — ceasefire violated. Netanyahu Independence Day address: Iran axis 'fighting for survival.' Pope Leo continues criticism of 'handful of tyrants' spending billions on war. Lebanon death toll 2,454; Iran 3,375 confirmed.
verificationNote: Compiled from Fortune, CNBC, Al Arabiya, Al Jazeera, Times of Israel, NPR. All sources verified.

text: IRGC POWER CONSOLIDATION: Euronews/Time/CNN all reporting IRGC commander Ahmad Vahidi now making military/political decisions alongside Mojtaba Khamenei. Pezeshkian blocked from appointments. AI-generated videos of absent Supreme Leader. US officials confirm 'absolute fracture' between negotiators and military. Iran has become 'a powerful militia with a state.'
verificationNote: Confirmed by Euronews, Time, CNN, Axios. IRGC power structure analysis from ISW and US intelligence.

text: ESPIONAGE: Two Israeli Air Force technicians from Tel Nof Airbase charged with spying for Iran during Operation Roaring Lion. Passed F-15 engine diagrams and pilot photographs. 8 more soldiers suspected of knowing without reporting. Part of '400% increase in Iranian recruitment attempts since Oct 2023.' 50+ espionage indictments total.
verificationNote: Confirmed by Jerusalem Post liveblog April 22.

text: JOURNALIST KILLED: Al Akhbar reporter Amal Khalil (43) confirmed dead in Israeli airstrike on al-Tiri village, southern Lebanon. Colleague Zeinab Faraj critically injured. CPJ called for rescue — Israeli drone dropped sound grenade on rescue teams, blocking access ~4 hours. 9th journalist killed in Lebanon this year. Lebanese PM Salam: 'war crime.'
verificationNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera, WaPo, CPJ, Xinhua, Naharnet. Al Akhbar confirmed employment.

text: PENTAGON: FY2027 budget request at $1.5 TRILLION — 42% increase, biggest since WWII. $74B for drones (3x), $30B for munitions, 785 Tomahawk missiles (up from 55 = 14x increase). Separately, Pentagon removed 15 wounded from casualty rolls without explanation — dropped from 428 to 413. Navy Secretary Phelan fired during active blockade; Hung Cao installed.
verificationNote: Budget from The Hill, Military.com. Casualty removal from The Intercept. Navy Sec from CNN.

text: DAY 55 ROUNDUP: Brent $103.38 (+29.5%), WTI $93.09 (+23.3%) — 4th straight gain. Gas $4.04. Vitol CEO: 600-700M bbl lost. IRGC power grab exposed. Journalist Amal Khalil killed in Lebanon. Hezbollah fires first time since Apr 16. Senate war powers vote #5 fails 46-51. Lebanon to request 1-month ceasefire extension at Thursday DC talks. New US sanctions: 22 targets. Iran Air resumes domestic flights. Trump: 'no time frame.'
verificationNote: Compiled from CNBC, CNN, Al Jazeera, Times of Israel, CBS News, Democracy Now, Xinhua. All verified.
